observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Prepare a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register or Taxpayer’s Index Card;

Furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list to serve as the basis in recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in the books.

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

Set-up RPT/SET Receivables in full amount based on the Certified List furnished by the Municipal Treasurer based on the AR submitted by the Municipal Assessor and not only on LGU’s share on RPT and SET Income;

Reconcile the variance between the RPT and SET Receivable to reflect the correct account balances at year-end.

We recommended, and the management agreed to prepare the BRS accurately and submit the same to COA Office with its corresponding Bank Statements or its equivalents, Debit/Credit Memoranda and Schedule of Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Assessor to make representation to the Provincial Assessor to notify/summon the RPU owners for reclassification of their RPUs to its actual use and revise the Tax Declaration.

Likewise, we recommended, and the Local Sanggunian agreed to review the Local Revenue Code and to consider the adoption of the National Building Code of the Philippines.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE direct the following:

All End-users shall submit PRs at the earliest period as indicated in their procurement timeline in the Project Procurement and Management Plan and/or Annual Procurement Plan.

The BAC Secretariat to strategize the consolidation of PRs received for the same nature or items and identify the most appropriate mode of procurement considering the thresholds amount to avoid stop splitting the PRs.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Procure its CSEs under the DBM-PS;

The BAC to secure CNAS or any documents to support the same when it desires to procure its CSE other than the DBM-PS; and

Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Law

We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure project implemented by administration, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of the agency; and

Afterwards, submit to COA the SWA, Inspection Report, pictures and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Observe prudence in cash management by introducing and implementing austerity measures on its daily operations and providing sufficient cash back-up for due and demandable obligations; and

Refrain from estimating sources of funds over and above the expected receipts and collections of the succeeding year to avoid appropriating unfunded disbursements for the current year.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct:

The Accountable Officers to issue demand letter to cause the immediate liquidation of the abovementioned cash advances. Otherwise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to withhold their salaries until the cash advances had been settled.

Ensure that no cash advances shall be made to officials and employees with outstanding balances in accordance with PD 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation, and the management agreed that the Local Sanggunian authorize the opening of depository account for SEF upon recommendation of the Municipal Treasurer duly approved by the Municipal Mayor to ensure that receipts and disbursements of SEF are monitored.

We recommended and Management agreed to undertake the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures as mentioned on the above cited regulations to avoid further deterioration of unserviceable assets.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Accounting Office to apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts and ensure proper classification of assets in the books; and

Designated GSO to update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties. Moreover, cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt and replace with ICS for issued semi-expandable property to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.

We recommended and Management agreed to appraise the vaccines, supplies and other equipment received without cost from DOH-ZPCHD to determine the amount for its proper recording in the books of accounts.

Moving forward, Management also agreed to present and disclose in the Notes to FS transactions received from outside sources of the same nature.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:
The MHRMO –
Prepare a Masterlist of earned leave credits of the officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and submit it to the Local Finance Committee (LFC) thru the Office of the Municipal Budget for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds;
Prepare the quarterly leave credits earned by officials and employees, with peso equivalent and supported with a copy of its respective leave card, as of every end of the quarter, starting CY 2025; and
Submit the Masterlist and the quarterly report of earned leave credits of officials and employees to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books subject to the availability of appropriation and funds certified by the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer.
The LFC to consider appropriating funds for its purpose prioritizing the retirable and elected officials upon receipt of the Masterlist of the earned leave credits as of December 31, 2024 from the MHRMO.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record in the books the liability, upon receipt of the Masterlist and the quarterly report of earned leave credits of officials and employees from the MHRMO, duly supported by a certification from the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer on the availability of appropriation and funds; and
Properly review the transactions and ensure that payments of leave monetization are recorded under Leave Benefits Expense, moving forward.

We recommended, and Management agreed to take the following actions:
The Inventory Committee to convene and conduct an appraisal of the current recoverable service amount of the unserviceable property, and subsequently submit the appraisal results to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books of accounts.
The MGSO to –
Conduct an investigation to identify and locate the accountable officer/s responsible for the missing PPE items. Once identified, require the said accountable officer/s to file a formal request to COA for relief from accountability; and
Submit a formal request for relief from accountability duly substantiated with the required supporting documents in connection with the fire incident that occurred in CY 2019.
Municipal Accountant to –
Recognize depreciation and compute the impairment loss of the unserviceable properties to ensure that the FS reflect their accurate and recoverable service amount, rather than their overstated carrying amount;
Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to derecognize the missing PPE items from the books of accounts upon receipt of the COA approved request/s for relief from accountability, and establish the corresponding receivable account/s from the identified accountable officer/s;
Prepare a JEV reclassifying the PPE items with acquisition costs below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold by preparing and recording the appropriate accounting entries, as suggested below; and
Ensure that a corresponding depreciation shall be recognized for PPE items whether actually used or not by the Municipality, for as long as they are available for use. To record the accumulated depreciation for the years 2023 and 2024, consider the suggested accounting entries:

We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize issued semi-expendable items thru the following suggested journal entries:
Moving forward, present in the NFS the required material disclosures on Inventory accounts as required under IPSAS 12, paragraph 47; and
Immediately recognize as expense the amount of semi-expendable properties upon issuance to end-user.
The MGSO to prepare and issue the ICS to the end-users who received the semi-expendable items to establish accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer –
Prepare and update a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year data of which should come from the RPT Account Register or Taxpayer’s Index Card;
Furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list to serve as the basis in recording the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables and its Deferred accounts in the books; and
Initiate the remedies provided under the Local Government Code to effectively enforce the collection of RPT and SET dues.
The Municipal Accountant –
Set-up the RPT and SET Receivables in full amount based on the Certified List furnished by the Municipal Treasurer duly reconciled with the Assessment Roll of the Municipal Assessor; and
Reconcile the variance noted between the ending balance of the SET Receivables and its Deferred accounts as per the supporting schedule and the corresponding balance reflected in the FS.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Accounting Office committed to maintain the complete set of GLs and SLs for the LGU’s accounts to uphold completeness and reliability of the financial records of the LGU.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Timely prepare and submit the monthly BRS for all LGU funds, duly supported by complete Bank Statements;
Utilize the generated bank statements from LBP weAccess, when the Bank Statements or Bank Snapshots are not immediately available; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the non-existing bank account duly supported by a confirmation from the bank.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue a memorandum for the conduct of the physical count of PPE and specifically relieve the members of the Inventory Committee of their regular duties and tasks until the physical count is completed.
We also recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:
The Inventory Committee to –
Ensure that the COA Auditor is invited to witness or notified on the conduct of inventory taking by the Municipality;
Prepare PIP and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking is properly and adequately planned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Conduct a comprehensive physical count of PPE based on the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Diligently and judiciously prepare the RPCPPE after the physical count based on the results of the ICFs following the actual physical count, rather than merely using it as a tool to reconcile the reported balances in the FS.
The MGSO/Property Unit to prepare and update the PCs based on the results of the physical inventory and to reconcile them with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the Municipality’s PPEs.
The Municipal Accountant to –
Update the PPELCs based on the results of the physical inventory and to reconcile them with MGSO/Property Unit records; and
Record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, supported with appropriate documents.
We recommended, and the management agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above-mentioned misstatements.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Prepare Deed of Donation and/or Memorandum of Agreement which serves as the basis for the derecognition of PPEs in the books of accounts and the recognition under the barangay books; and

Take up the necessary adjusting entries to accurately reflect the accounts affected.
We recommended, and the management agreed to draw JEV to effect the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended, and Management agreed to prepare adjusting entry to record the assets in the books of accounts by debiting CIP – Buildings and Other Structures account and crediting Prior Period Adjustment to correct the errors made.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize depreciation by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Accumulated Depreciation-Power Supply Systems by an amount equivalent to ₱20,819.17

Ensure that all PPEs subject to depreciation have been provided with a depreciation expense

Install an internal control with regards to counter-checking the details logged in the prepared excel file documents and ensure that complete and accurate data have been provided.
We recommended, and the management agreed to follow the accounting entries prepared on the MNGAS pending the implementation/use of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Require the concerned SDO to immediately liquidate the said cash advances;

Refrain from the practice of allowing different AOs to handle cash disbursements aside from the SDO; and

Ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
We recommended, and the management agreed to refrain from charging ineligible expenditures against the 20 per cent DF as provided under item 3.2.5 of the JMC above.

We reiterated our recommendation, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, the Chairperson of the LSB to convene immediately to discuss and approve the planning and budgeting of the SEF for the incoming budget year taking into consideration its priority programs, projects and activities for the schools.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to strictly follow the procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items and maintenance of stock cards.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS properly in accordance with the forementioned regulations, moving forward, reconcile the deficiencies noted, and submit a corrected copy of the BRS and JEV to the COA for any adjustments effected in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to strictly follow the procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items and maintenance of stock cards.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the perpetual inventory method in recording of procured goods and record the same initially as inventory upon purchase, and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuance to the requesting units duly supported with Summary of Supplies and MateriaIs Issued, RIS and any other appropriate documents supporting the issuance.

We recommended and Management agreed to appraise the vaccines, supplies and other equipment received without cost from DOH-ZPCHD to determine the amount for its proper recording in the books of accounts.

Moving forward, Management also agreed to present and disclose in the Notes to FS transactions received from outside sources of the same nature.
We recommended and Management agreed to request the LSB to convene immediately to start the planning and budgeting of the SEF for the incoming budget year taking into consideration its priority PPAs.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to make representation to the Local Sanggunian for authorization of opening depository account for SEF in order that funds may be saved/utilized only according to its purpose.

The Local Chief Executive to direct the Inventory Committee to exert their best efforts in completing the required physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties not included in the previous inventory report.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to reconcile the PPE items recorded in the GF books with that of the RPCPPE prepared by the inventory committee.

Management to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers listed above for us to complete our verification and audit of the said transactions. Likewise, the submission thereof includes the list of recipients of relief goods and/or distribution sheets with their address and signature for the specific supplies received such as canned goods, noodles, rice and other relief goods distributed.
We also recommended that Management to improve their monitoring of operational requirements like the submission of PSCP.
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.

The Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

b.	Municipal GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to exert their best efforts in completing the required physical count of PPE owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties which are not included in the previous inventory report.

Also, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously reconcile the PPE items recorded in the GF books against the RPCPPE.	

We recommended and Management agreed to undertake the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures as mentioned on the above cited regulations to avoid further deterioration of unserviceable assets.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Accounting Office to apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts and ensure proper classification of assets in the books; and  

Designated GSO to update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties. Moreover, cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt and replace with Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for issued semi-expandable property to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation procedures on the liability accounts with negative (debit) balances so that the Financial Statement be more reflective of the actual financial position of the agency as of the reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the Financial Statement including the necessary information to be incorporated in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer perform regular reconciliation between the cashbook and accounting records to arrive at an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank balance as of the given period.
We recommended and Management agreed to record future transactions in its appropriate journals as mentioned above to fast-track the preparation of the financial records and/or reports.

We recommended and Management agreed to create a Monitoring Team/Committee in coordination with Municipal Agriculture Office and the PESO Manager to take charge in the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of PPAs handled by different NGOs/POs which shall not be limited to regular inspection of the project.

We also recommended and Management agreed to submit to COA the financial reports and records of the NGOs/POs as of December 31, 2022 for further audit verification.

We recommended and Management agreed to create regulatory policies and/or internal guidelines relating to donations which clearly define the eligibility of specific beneficiaries and the limitation of grant per eligible person.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to proposed to the LDRRM Council to identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years’ unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in its prescribed format as duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within 15 days after the end of each month.

We recommended and Management agreed to fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign to be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from open burning of solid waste and strictly comply to various prohibitions of R.A. 9003.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to:

Include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit; and

Conduct gender audit on its agency using GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to administer HGDG tool particularly the PIMME and/or FIMME checklist on all of the major PPAs attributed to GAD in the GPB to determine the actual expenditures of the major PPAs to be attributed to GAD during the preparation of the GAD AR.

a. Required the Inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief Executive and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor. 

b. The GSO/MT and Accountant to prepare Property Cards and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, based on the actual PPE counted to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the property and accounting records.

1.6 We recommended the Local Chief  Executive  to  direct  the  Inventory
       Committee to exert their best  efforts  in  completing  the  required
       physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU  and  update
       the RPCPPE with the data of those  properties  not  included  in  the
       previous inventory report.
  1.7 Moreover, direct the MA to prepare adjusting entries to reconcile the
       PPE items recorded in the GF books with that of the  RPCPPE  prepared
       by the inventory committee.

2.7       We recommended the LCE to create and Inventory  Committee  with
       adequate number of members and representatives  from  the  Accounting
       and Property Division/Unit to complete the conduct  of  the  physical
       inventory-taking in accordance with the procedures  of  COA  Circular
       No. 202-006 dated January 31, 2020 which is the one-time cleansing of
       PPE Account Balances.

3.11      We  recommended  that  management  to  submit  immediately  the
       disbursement  vouchers  listed  above  for   us   to   complete   our
       verification and  audit  of  the  said  transactions.  Likewise,  the
       submission thereof includes the list of recipients  of  relief  goods
       and/or distribution sheets with their address and signature  for  the
       specific supplies received such as canned goods,  noodles,  rice  and
       other relief goods distributed.

4.9 To strengthen the risk management capabilities of the LGU and  enable
       it to manage its vulnerabilities in the event of  disruptive  events,
       we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  institutionalize
       the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the  following
       activities:


       a.   to conduct risk assessment;
       b.   to identify critical processes and functions;
       c.   to determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
       d.   to conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
       e.   to formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on  critical
           processes and functions and its related steps to be followed  to
           eliminate,  if  not  mitigate,  the  impact  of  the  determined
           disruptions following the prescribed template and be  guided  by
           the PSCP guidebook; and
       f.   to submit plan  to  the  MDRRMO  for  review,  to  the  LCE  for
           approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan  for  the  passage  of  a
           resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.


  4.10      We also recommended that Management to improve their monitoring
       of operational requirements like the submission of PSCP.

5.7 We recommended that the LGU submit the contracts and purchase  orders
       after its perfection as required under  COA  Circular  No.  2009-001,
       together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes  of  the
       proceedings, abstracts, and such  other  documents  peculiar  to  the
       contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary
       in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation
       thereof.

6.10        We   recommended   that   management    ensure    that    the
       unexpended/unobligated balances for DRRM-MOOE and Quick Response Fund
       are transferred to the TF at year-end in conformity  with  the  above
       cited provisions.

7.4 We recommended that management to fully institutionalize  the  LDRRMO
       by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to  assist  the  DRRM
       Officer in the administration and training,  research  and  planning,
       and operations and  warning  to  assure  implementation  of  plan  of
       actions  and  measures  related  to  disaster  risk   reduction   and
       management.

8.5 We recommended that:


       •    Management to establish responsibility center for GFPS under the
           Office of the Mayor;


       •    The MA provide RC code in the SLs for the manual accounting; and


       •    The MBO to provide RC code in the eBudget or (Budget if manual).

9.9 We recommended that management include in its Annual Investment  Plan
       (AIP) programs, projects  and  activities  that  generate  additional
       income to finance more valuable Programs/Projects and  Activities  of
       the LGU. Additionally, update the Revenue Code of the LGU pursuant to
       Section 191 of R.A. No. 7160.

10.8      We recommended that the local finance committee composed of the
       Budget Officer, Treasurer and Planning and  Development  Officer  who
       are responsible in the preparation of the annual budget are  required
       to accurately identify its activity  consistent  with  the  Chart  of
       Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009.


  10.9       Likewise,  the  MA  to  specifically  record  the  transaction
       according to the appropriate expense found in the Chart  of  Accounts
       under the same COA guidelines.

12.6      We recommended that management to submit disbursement  vouchers
       on progress billing and its supporting documents immediately  for  us
       to complete the post-audit of the said  transactions.  Likewise,  the
       Municipal Accountant being the Internal Auditor of the LGU, to  check
       the completeness of the supporting documents for  every  disbursement
       of government funds.

13.9      We recommended that the management require  the  contractor  to
       explain the cause of delay.  Likewise, require the contractor to fast-
       track the implementation  of  the  project.  Also,  conduct  periodic
       inspection,  monitoring  and  analysis   whether   such   performance
       coincides with the timelines, if  not,  consider  the  imposition  of
       liquidated damages for the  unjustified  delays  in  accordance  with
       Annex E of the RIRR of RA 9184.
The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries when needed.

Management to submit and provide the necessary documents on the transactions presented as to the procurement and other documents containing information about the description of the items, the quantity of goods distributed, signatures of the recipient and any documents or its equivalent to support that all listed beneficiaries actually received it, for proper accounting.

Likewise, provide the audit team the reports and inventory list of all procured, donated items and unused/unissued goods, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

Management to: 

A.	Local Chief Executive 

1.	Require the department heads to submit RCPPE per office to fast tract the physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU.

B.	Members of the Inventory Committee:

1.	After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office has to conduct reconciliation to establish the existence, status or conditions, and the whereabouts of the assets;

2.	Moreover, those missing PPEs established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.

C.	Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant

1.	To maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.

The Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

a.	Inventory Committee

1.	Conduct and inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;

2.	Prepare the Report on Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU (Annex C of the Circular)

b.\tMunicipal General Services Officer

1.	Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

2.	Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

c.\tMunicipal Engineer

1.	Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects

d.\tMunicipal Accountant

1.	Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;

2.	Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;

3.	Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and

4.	Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:

1. To direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Taxpayers’ Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer;

2. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the duly Certified List of Taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant; and

3. The Municipal Accountant to set-up at the beginning of the year the estimated RPT/SET Receivables thru the preparation of a JEV.

We recommended that the LSB convene immediately to start the planning and budgeting of the SEF for the incoming budget year taking into consideration the priority needs of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education as detailed in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, series of 2017 in order serve the purpose of the fund it was created.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required reports on time as mandated by the above regulations.

We recommended that the agency submit, within seven (7) days after the grant of the cash advance, the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to the COA Audit Team Leader in a sealed envelope, as required in the above cited provisions.

a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct all offices to conduct their own physical count and submit the required reports to the Property Officer for validation/consolidation purposes. This action will assist the inventory committee to comply its task easily.

b. Required the Inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief Executive and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor. 

c. The GSO/MT and Accountant to prepare Property Cards and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, based on the actual PPE counted to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the property and accounting records.

a. The management to require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Service Officer at the end of accounting period.

b. Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its acquired assets.

The Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at the beginning of the year the estimated receivable of RPT and SET.

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit all the required reports within the deadline mandated by existing rules and regulations to ensure ample time for the audit and also to provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making.

The municipality’s GAD focal point system conduct to gender analysis and assessment so that they can formulate policies and plans that will address gender issues in their LGU and mainstream the programs, projects and activities to attain gender equality. Furthermore, require the SB members to formulate and enact GAD Codes that will support its efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, economic empowerment and gender- responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
observations.reason: Gender issues not totally addressed by GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
The management to:

A.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

B.	Ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for MOOE and Quick Response Fund of LDRRMF are transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end in conformity with Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002.

C.	Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registry for the appropriations, allotments, and obligations of local disaster risk reduction and management fund specifically on 30 per cent QRF, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay.

The Municipality to implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of MRF, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its jurisdiction.
The management to immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that fines, penalties, or abatements may not be imposed on the agency.

observations.reason: No reports submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Management to immediately create the Solid Waste Management Board by an issuance of an Executive Order and the Board to prepare the Municipality's Solid Waste Management Plan to have a permanent and long- term plan in the protection of its environment and maintaining clean surroundings, as envision in R.A. 9003 and its implementing rules and regulation.

1.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries when needed.

2.9	We recommended that management submit and provide the necessary documents on the transactions presented as to the procurement and other documents containing information about the description of the items, the quantity of goods distributed, signatures of the recipient and any documents or its equivalent to support that all listed beneficiaries actually received it, for proper accounting.
2.10	Likewise, provide the audit team the reports and inventory list of all procured, donated items and unused/unissued goods, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

3.9 	We recommended the following to management:
Local Chief Executive
Require the department heads to submit RCPPE per office to fast tract the physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU.
Members of the Inventory Committee:
After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office has to conduct reconciliation to establish the existence, status or conditions, and the whereabouts of the assets;
Moreover, those missing PPEs established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
To maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.

4.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Inventory Committee
Conduct and inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;
Prepare the Report on Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU (Annex C of the Circular)
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

5.8	We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
To direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Taxpayers’ Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer;
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the duly Certified List of Taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant to set-up at the beginning of the year the estimated RPT/SET Receivables thru the preparation of a JEV.

6.7	We recommended that the LSB convene immediately to start the planning and budgeting of the SEF for the incoming budget year taking into consideration the priority needs of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education as detailed in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, series of 2017 in order serve the purpose of the fund it was created.
7.6	We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required reports on time as mandated by the above regulations.

8.5	We recommended that the agency submit, within seven (7) days after the grant of the cash advance, the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to the COA Audit Team Leader in a sealed envelope, as required in the above cited provisions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall direct all offices to conduct their own physical count and submit the required reports to the Property Officer for validation/consolidation purposes. This action will assist the inventory committee to comply its task easily.

Require the Inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief 	Executive and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.


The GSO/MT and Accountant maintain Property Cards and Equipment Ledger Cards, 	respectively, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the property and accounting 	records.

We recommend that the management require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. 

Likewise, require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Service Officer at the end of accounting period.

Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its acquired assets.

We recommend to the local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at the beginning of the year the estimated receivable of RPT and SET.

We recommend to the management:
	
Require the GAD focal point to submit yearly the manual GAD Plan budget of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial Government and DILG Regional Offices for its review and approval

Require the GAD focal point to submit GAD Accomplishment Reports to the DILG Regional Office for review and consolidation in order to determine and evaluate if such are align to women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We recommend that the Municipality implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of Materials Recovery Facility/s, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its jurisdiction.

Management should immediately create its Solid Waste Management Board by issuance of an Executive Order and the Board shall prepare the Municipality's Solid Waste Management Plan to have a permanent and long- term plan in the protection of its environment and maintaining clean surroundings, as envision in R.A. 9003 and its implementing rules and regulation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct all offices to conduct their own physical count and submit the required reports to the Property Officer for validation/consolidation purposes. This action will assist the inventory committee to comply its task easily.
Required the Inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief Executive and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.
The GSO/MT and Accountant to prepare Property Cards and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, based on the actual PPE counted to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the property and accounting records;

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Service Officer at the end of accounting period.
Required the recognition of the Road Networks of the municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its acquired assets.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at the beginning of the year the estimated receivable of RPT and SET.

Recommendations:
Required the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit all the required reports within the deadline mandated by existing rules and regulations to ensure ample time for the audit and also to provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality’s GAD focal point system conduct gender analysis and assessment so that they can formulate policies and plans that will address gender issues in their LGU and mainstream the programs, projects and activities to attain gender equality. Furthermore, require the SB members to formulate and enact GAD Codes that will support its efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, economic empowerment and gender- responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for MOOE and Quick Response Fund of LDRRMF are transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end in conformity with Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registry for the appropriations, allotments, and obligations of local disaster risk reduction and management fund specifically on 30 per cent QRF, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of MRF, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its jurisdiction.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that fines, penalties, or abatements may not be imposed on the agency.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional personnel for the Office of the Municipal Accountant to ensure the preparation and submission of all financial reports particularly the bank reconciliation statements as required under PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.

Also, we recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant locate and gather all required statements/reports/documents necessary for the preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.

We recommend that the management direct the municipal accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to all accounts of the municipality for proper control and monitoring of agency’s transactions and financial records.

We recommend that the Municipality submit the contracts and purchase orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Required the recognition of the Road Network of the Municipality as mandated under COA. Management should immediately utilize the facility to achieve the purpose for which it was constructed and realize the aim of the funding agency to alleviate the plight of the fishing industry sector of the Municipality.

Required the recognition of the Road Network of the Municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its  acquired assets. Likewise, we enjoin all concern such as the Municipal Engineer Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer/Municipal Treasurer to work hand in hand to attain the objective of the Circular as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional personnel for the Office of the Municipal Accountant to ensure the preparation and submission of all financial reports particularly the bank reconciliation statements as required under PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.
Also, we recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant locate and gather all required statements/reports/documents necessary for the preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall direct all offices to conduct their own physical count and submit the required reports to the Property Officer for validation/consolidation purposes. This action will assist the inventory committee to comply its task easily.
Require the Inventory committee to prepare and submit the report to the Local Chief 	Executive and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.
The GSO/MT and Accountant maintain Property Cards and Equipment Ledger Cards, 	respectively, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the property and accounting 	records.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Service Officer at the end of accounting period.
Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its acquired assets.

We recommended that the management direct the municipal accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to all accounts of the municipality for proper control and monitoring of agency’s transactions and financial records.

We recommended to the local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at the beginning of the year the estimated receivable of RPT and SET.

Establish proper coordination between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Budget and Municipal Development Offices regarding of the preparation of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by the municipality.
Require the concerned office  to submit at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with the provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013
Require the concerned office to conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to its status of implementation and submit regularly Quarterly Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to COA Office  and
Provide our office of any conditions and revisions or revisions or charges to the projects to be implemented for our validation and monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipality submit the contracts and purchase orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended to the management:
a.   Require the GAD focal point to submit yearly the manual GAD Plan budget of the   Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial Government and DILG Regional Offices for its review and approval
b.   Require the GAD focal point to submit GAD Accomplishment Reports to the DILG Regional Office for review and consolidation in order to determine and evaluate if such are align to womens empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended that the Municipality implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of Materials Recovery Facility/s, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its jurisdiction.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statement on a regular and timely basis; and likewise require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain from the bank the snapshots of each account right after the end of each month as a basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation.

Require the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Assessment Roll to Municipal Treasurer; and for the latter to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due thereon ; and finally for the Municipal Accountants to set-up, thru  the JEV preparation at the estimated receivables of RPT and SET

Management should immediately create its Solid Wasted Management Board by issuance of an Executive Order and the Board shall prepare the Municipality's Solid Waste Management Plan to have a permanent and long- term plan in the protection of its environment and maintaining clean surroundings, as envision in R.A. 9003 and its implementing rules and regulation.

Management should immediately utilize the facility to achieve the purpose for which it was constructed and realize the aim of the funding agency to alleviate the plight of the fishing industry sector of the Municipality.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow the guidelines in the submission of  contracts and purchase orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-01. Likewise , all deliveries should be properly documented by the preparing  the Acceptance and Inspection Reports

The Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of tax payers at the beginning of the year in order for the latter to book up the estimated receivables of RPT and SET.

Require  the  Municipal  Accountant   to   prepare   the   bank
reconciliation statement on a regular and timely basis;  and  likewise
require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain from the bank the  snapshots
of each account right after the end of each month  as  basis  for  the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation.

Require  the  Municipal  Assessor  to  prepare  and  submit  the
Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer;  and  for  the  latter  to
prepare the certified list of taxpayers and the  amount  due  thereon;
and finally, for the Municipal Accountant  to  set-up,  thru  the  JEV
preparation at the beginning of the year the estimated receivables of
RPT and SET.

Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the Municipality
as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect  all  its
acquired assets.


         Likewise, we enjoin all concern such as the Municipal  Engineer,
Municipal  Accountant  and  the  General   Service   Officer/Municipal
Treasurer to work hand in hand to attain the objective of the Circular
as soon as possible.

Management should immediately create its Solid Waste  Management
Board by issuance of an Executive Order and the  Board  shall  prepare
the municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan to have a permanent and
long-term plan in the protection of its  environment  and  maintaining
clean surroundings, as envision in  R.A.  9003  and  its  implementing
rules and regulations.

Management should immediately utilize the  facility  to  achieve
the purpose for which it was constructed and realize the  aim  of  the
funding agency to alleviate the plight of the fishing industry  sector
of the Municipality.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow  the  guidelines
in the submission of contracts and purchase orders as  required  under
COA Circular No. 2009-01.  Likewise, all deliveries should be properly
documented by preparing the Acceptance and Inspection Report.

We are reiterating our recommendation that liquidation on cash advances for confidential fund be submitted to the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit and if not, settlement thereof is desired.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow the guidelines in the submission of contracts and purchase orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We recommend that management should require accounting department to prepare and submit reports on a timely basis which necessitate that the same is submitted not later than the 20th day of the month following the month reported.

We recommend that management requires the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) on a daily basis, in order for the latter to prepare a time financial reports.  On the other hand, all checks should only be released by the Municipal Treasurer or authorized personnel under the department to avoid misplacement if not loss of paid disbursement vouchers.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow the guidelines on procurement of common used supplies as mandated under Executive Order No. 40 as reiterated under Administrative Order No. 17.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the bank reconciliation on a timely basis and avoid further delay and submit to the auditor for review.   Such reconciliation, should be accompanied with the bank statements together with the negotiated or cleared checks.
observations.reason: Prepared but not on a monthly basis and incomplete.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that liquidation on cash advances for confidential fund be submitted to the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit.  If the same could not be liquidated, settlement thereof is desired.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to Report on the Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2015, and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher in setting up of the Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a Report of Collections and Deposit and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the income of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Collection and Deposit as a basis of recording of collections and likewise, submit to the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms together with its Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.
	
	On the other hand, require all collectors to prepare every end of the month their respective Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same on time to the Municipal Treasurer as a basis for preparation of the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.

We recommend that the Treasury personnel should have a copy of the Local Revenue Code and be made familiar on the content of the Code, so that targeted revenue can be attained.

Likewise, considering that the Code was passed in 1994, it maybe timely to review the provisions thereof.

We recommend that the GFPS should not only be constituted but should be functioning by following the guidelines set forth under PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 and the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 issued and signed by the PCW, DILG, DBM, and the NEDA.

Likewise to improve its understanding and as part of improving awareness on GAD, an in-house seminars and agency sponsored symposium maybe considered.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to Report on the Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2015, and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher in setting up of the Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant follows the guidelines in the preparation of report and maintenance of record.

The Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of tax payers at the beginning of the year in order for the latter to book up the estimated receivables of RPT and SET.

We are reiterating our recommendation that liquidation  on
      cash advances for confidential fund be submitted to the Office of  the
      Chairman, Commission on  Audit  and  if  not,  settlement  thereof  is
      desired.

The Municipal Treasurer to submit to  the  Municipal  Accountant
      the certified list of tax payers at the beginning of the year in order
      for the latter to book up the estimated receivables of RPT and SET.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow  the  guidelines
      in the submission of contracts and purchase orders as  required  under
      COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We  recommend  that   management   should   require   accounting
      department to prepare and submit  reports  on  a  timely  basis  which
      necessitate that the same is submitted not later than the 20th day  of
      the month following the month reported.

We  recommend  that  management  requires  the  Municipal
      Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report
      of Checks Issued (RCI) on a daily basis, in order for  the  latter  to
      prepare a time financial reports.   On  the  other  hand,  all  checks
      should only be released  by  the  Municipal  Treasurer  or  authorized
      personnel under the department to avoid misplacement if  not  loss  of
      paid disbursement vouchers.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow  the  guidelines
      on procurement of common used supplies  as  mandated  under  Executive
      Order No. 40 as reiterated under Administrative Order No. 17.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the bank reconciliation on a timely basis and avoid further delay and submit to the auditor for review.   Such reconciliation, should be accompanied with the bank statements together with the negotiated or cleared checks.
observations.reason: Prepared but not on a monthly basis and incomplete.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that liquidation on cash advances for confidential fund be submitted to the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit.  If the same could not be liquidated, settlement thereof is desired.
observations.reason: P7,350,000.00 was refunded in 2016 with remaining balance of P9,700,00.00.  Due to its materiality, this was reiterated.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a Report of Collections and Deposit and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the income of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Collection and Deposit as a basis of recording of collections and likewise, submit to the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms together with its Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.

	On the other hand, require all collectors to prepare every end of the month their respective Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same on time to the Municipal Treasurer as a basis for preparation of the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.

We recommend that the Treasury personnel should have a copy of the Local Revenue Code and be made familiar on the content of the Code, so that targeted revenue can be attained.

Likewise, considering that the Code was passed in 1994, it maybe timely to review the provisions thereof.

The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer who are responsible in the preparation of the budget plan are required to properly identify its activity in conjunction with the Chart of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2004-008.
